heading: Election to discontinue participation: one-year opportunity after second anniversary of commencement of payment of retired pay
label: 1448a Election to discontinue participation: one-year opportunity after second anniversary of commencement of payment of retired pay
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–85, div. A, title VI, § 641(c)111 Stat. 1799Nov. 18, 1997“, United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall take effect 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Section 1448a of title 10Nov. 18, 1997

Transition Provision for Current Participants, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–85, div. A, title VI, § 641(b)111 Stat. 1798Nov. 18, 1997“Notwithstanding the limitation on the time for making an election under , United States Code (as added by subsection (a)), that is specified in subsection (a) of such section, a participant in the Survivor Benefit Plan under subchapter II of chapter 73 of such title may make an election in accordance with that section within one year after the effective date of that section under subsection (c) [set out as an Effective Date note above] if the second anniversary of the commencement of payment of retired pay to the participant precedes that effective date.”section 1448a of title 10

A, title VI, § 641(b)111 Stat. 1798Nov. 18, 1997“Notwithstanding the limitation on the time for making an election under , United States Code (as added by subsection (a)), that is specified in subsection (a) of such section, a participant in the Survivor Benefit Plan under subchapter II of chapter 73 of such title may make an election in accordance with that section within one year after the effective date of that section under subsection (c) [set out as an Effective Date note above] if the second anniversary of the commencement of payment of retired pay to the participant precedes that effective date.”section 1448a of title 10

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: A participant in the Plan may, subject to the provisions of this section, elect to discontinue participation in the Plan at any time during the one-year period beginning on the second anniversary of the date on which payment of retired pay to the participant commences.

children.heading: .—Concurrence required
children.label: 1 .—Concurrence required
children.text: A married participant may not (except as provided in paragraph (2)) make an election under subsection (a) without the concurrence of the participant’s spouse.

children.heading: .—Exceptions
children.label: 2 .—Exceptions
children.text: A participant may make such an election without the concurrence of the participant’s spouse by establishing to the satisfaction of the Secretary concerned that one of the conditions specified in exists.section 1448(a)(3)(C) of this title

children.heading: .—Form of concurrence
children.label: 3 .—Form of concurrence
children.text: The concurrence of a spouse under paragraph (1) shall be made in such written form and shall contain such information as may be required under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Election When Former Spouse Coverage in Effect
children.label: c .—Limitation on Election When Former Spouse Coverage in Effect
children.text: The limitation set forth in applies to an election to discontinue participation in the Plan under subsection (a).section 1450(f)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Withdrawal of Election To Discontinue
children.label: d .—Withdrawal of Election To Discontinue
children.text: applies to an election under subsection (a).Section 1448(b)(1)(D) of this title

children.heading: .—Consequences of Discontinuation
children.label: e .—Consequences of Discontinuation
children.text: applies to an election under subsection (a).Section 1448(b)(1)(E) of this title

children.heading: .—Notice to Affected Beneficiaries
children.label: f .—Notice to Affected Beneficiaries
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall notify any former spouse or other natural person previously designated under of an election to discontinue participation under subsection (a).section 1448(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Effective Date of Election
children.label: g .—Effective Date of Election
children.text: An election under subsection (a) is effective as of the first day of the first calendar month following the month in which the election is received by the Secretary concerned.

children.heading: .—Inapplicability of Irrevocability Provisions
children.label: h .—Inapplicability of Irrevocability Provisions
children.text: Paragraphs (4)(B) and (5)(C) of do not apply to prevent an election under subsection (a).section 1448(a) of this title
